description: Feature ID: g5affCoi The `#status-message` element inside the `#bottom-status` footer bar can display long text that wraps to the next line, breaking the fixed 30px height layout. Add CSS text-overflow ellipsis handling so long status text is truncated with "‚Ä¶" instead of wrapping. **Current behavior:** Long status messages wrap onto multiple lines, overflowing the 30px footer height. **Desired behavior:** Long status messages are truncated with an ellipsis (`‚Ä¶`) and stay on a single line within the footer. **Implementation:** In `web/static/css/main.css`, add the following CSS properties to the `#bottom-status` rule (or as a new `#status-message` rule): - `white-space: nowrap` ‚Äî prevent text from wrapping - `overflow: hidden` ‚Äî hide overflowing text - `text-overflow: ellipsis` ‚Äî show "‚Ä¶" when text is truncated The most appropriate place is a new `#status-message` rule since the `#bottom-status` footer is a flex container and the span inside it should handle its own overflow. The `#status-message` span needs: ```css #status-message { white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; min-width: 0; } ``` The `min-width: 0` is needed because flex children don't shrink below their content size by default ‚Äî this allows the span to shrink and trigger ellipsis. **Acceptance criteria:** - The `#status-message` span never wraps text to a second line - When the status text is too long for the available width, it is truncated with an ellipsis ("‚Ä¶") - The `#bottom-status` footer maintains its fixed 30px height regardless of status text length - Short status messages display normally with no truncation **Technical notes:** - File to modify: `web/static/css/main.css` (add rules near the existing `#bottom-status` block around line 1293) - This is a CSS-only change ‚Äî no JavaScript or TypeScript modifications needed - Tests should NOT be added ‚Äî this is a purely visual/styling fix
planPoints: 1. Add `#status-message` CSS rule after the `#bottom-status` block in `web/static/css/main.css` ‚Äî added `white-space: nowrap`, `overflow: hidden`, `text-overflow: ellipsis`, and `min-width: 0` rules at line 1305
title: Truncate long status text with ellipsis
summary: Added CSS text-overflow handling to the `#status-message` element in `web/static/css/main.css` to prevent long status text from wrapping and breaking the fixed 30px footer height. The new rule applies `white-space: nowrap`, `overflow: hidden`, `text-overflow: ellipsis`, and `min-width: 0` to truncate long messages with an ellipsis while keeping them on a single line. This CSS-only change ensures the footer layout remains stable regardless of status message length.

conversationHistory.content: I'll start by reading the feature file to understand what needs to be built.Now let me find and read the CSS file:
Now I have everything I need. Let me append the plan and implement the change.

Now mark the step as done and add the summary:The implementation is complete. Here's what was done:

**CSS change in `web/static/css/main.css`:** Added a new `#status-message` rule right after the existing `#bottom-status` block (at line 1305) with four properties:

- `white-space: nowrap` ‚Äî prevents text wrapping
- `overflow: hidden` ‚Äî hides overflowing content
- `text-overflow: ellipsis` ‚Äî shows "‚Ä¶" when truncated
- `min-width: 0` ‚Äî allows the flex child to shrink below its content size, enabling the ellipsis to trigger

This ensures the `#bottom-status` footer maintains its fixed 30px height regardless of status text length, with long messages cleanly truncated with an ellipsis.
